next_steps: Send pricing, graphics, and testimonial for executive meeting
Schedule deep-dive call on pricing structure and product library cleanup
Establish mutual action plan with phased implementation

jtbd.job: Migrate off DealHub to a HubSpot-native CPQ without disrupting active quoting
jtbd.context: DealHub contract ending in 8 weeks. Current DealHub implementation requires a dedicated agency to manage because the tool is too convoluted for internal administration. CFO unhappy with ongoing agency cost.
jtbd.desired_outcome: Self-manageable quoting system inside HubSpot that the RevOps team can administer without external agency dependency

jtbd.job: Clean up a product library polluted by multiple migrations and ad-hoc SKU creation
jtbd.context: 800+ products in HubSpot from three separate Salesforce migrations plus a historical pattern of creating unique SKUs per deal. DealHub's one-way sync to HubSpot's product table prevents cleanup while DealHub is active.
jtbd.desired_outcome: A curated, minimal set of MVP SKUs that sales reps can quote from without confusion, with legacy SKUs hidden but preserved for historical deal integrity

jtbd.job: Accurately calculate and report ARR across new deals, expansions, and renewals
jtbd.context: Line items written back from DealHub are wrong, combining term and quantity in ways that triple ARR figures. Ramp-up deals compound the problem. Manual correction requires reading every contract PDF for billing terms buried in notes.
jtbd.desired_outcome: Formulaic pricing that writes correct line items directly in HubSpot so ARR rolls up accurately without manual reconciliation

pains.pain: DealHub creates a data silo that disconnects quoting from HubSpot, producing incorrect line items
pains.trigger: Every quote synced from DealHub writes back line items with wrong term/quantity combinations. 12,000+ line items need review. None have start or end dates.
pains.cost: RevOps leader manually reviewing every closed-won deal since January to fix line items. ARR reporting is unreliable (figures tripled in some cases). Finance cannot trust deal data.
pains.urgency_reason: Active problem consuming significant RevOps hours right now. Every day of delay compounds the data quality issue.

pains.pain: DealHub is too convoluted to manage internally, requiring a paid agency partner
pains.trigger: Any configuration change or quoting workflow update requires engaging a DealHub-specific agency. Internal team cannot self-serve.
pains.cost: Ongoing agency fees that the CFO is unhappy about. Dependency on external partner for routine operations. No internal capability building.
pains.urgency_reason: CFO is actively dissatisfied. Agency will off-board when DealHub contract ends, so replacement must be self-manageable.

pains.pain: 800+ SKUs from multiple migrations create confusion and quoting errors
pains.trigger: Three Salesforce migrations plus historical practice of creating unique SKUs per deal. Many SKUs are duplicates or inactive but cannot be cleaned up without affecting historical deal line items.
pains.cost: Sales reps face a bloated product catalog that confuses product selection. RevOps spending nights doing forensic audits of SKU history.
pains.urgency_reason: Must be resolved before new CPQ implementation but can be addressed in parallel with migration planning

pains.pain: HubSpot ARR calculation breaks on ramp-up deals and multi-year terms
pains.trigger: Billing start and end dates are set correctly but ARR rolls up every annual line item on the quote, tripling the figure for multi-year ramp deals.
pains.cost: Finance cannot rely on HubSpot ARR. RevOps building page-long formula fields as workarounds. Manual reconciliation against contract PDFs.
pains.urgency_reason: Third customer call this week with same problem per Quotivity sales engineer. Universal pain across HubSpot mid-market.

pains.pain: Expansion and renewal deals require manual reconciliation with no co-terming automation
pains.trigger: When a customer expands mid-term, the co-termed expansion must be manually folded into the renewal. Finance discovers mismatches only when renewals are triggered.
pains.cost: Finance manually cross-referencing expansion and renewal deals. Risk of billing errors on renewals. Process breaks when RevOps is unavailable.
pains.urgency_reason: Ongoing operational pain that will intensify as customer base grows. Waiting on HubSpot contracts object beta.

pains.pain: DealHub one-way sync prevents product library cleanup in HubSpot
pains.trigger: Any changes made to products in HubSpot are overwritten on the next DealHub sync. Cannot clean up the product table while DealHub is active.
pains.cost: Paralysis on data cleanup. RevOps is 'stymied' (her word). Cleanup can only begin after DealHub is fully decommissioned, compressing the migration timeline.
pains.urgency_reason: Blocking prerequisite for any CPQ migration. Creates hard dependency on DealHub off-boarding timing.

workflow_reality.current_process: Quotes are built in DealHub, which syncs one-way back to HubSpot's product table and line items. A DealHub-specific agency manages configuration changes. RevOps manually reviews and corrects line items on closed-won deals. ARR is calculated via custom formula fields on deal properties because HubSpot's native ARR calculation is unreliable. Contract terms are manually extracted from PDF notes. Expansions and renewals are handled as completely separate processes with manual reconciliation.
tools_in_use: Excel pricing calculator (pricing logic, tab-per-product structure)
The Operations Company / TOC (RevOps agency for HubSpot projects and migrations)
Contract PDFs (ARR terms stored in document notes)
brittleness_indicators: Single RevOps person managing entire quoting infrastructure
12,000 line items needing manual review and correction
No start or end dates on any line items
ARR figures tripled due to incorrect rollup logic
Product library bloated from three Salesforce migrations with no cleanup possible while DealHub syncs
Contract terms buried in PDF notes rather than structured data
RevOps leader working nights doing forensic audits of deal data
Agency dependency for routine DealHub changes

evaluated.name: DealHub
evaluated.what_they_liked: Handled complex pricing scenarios including tiered packages, module selection, and professional services add-ons.
evaluated.why_they_are_leaving: Too convoluted to manage internally. Requires a dedicated agency partner for any changes. One-way sync to HubSpot creates a data silo with incorrect line items. CFO unhappy with ongoing agency costs. Implementation was not done carefully, compounding data problems.
evaluated.key_quote: That's very much the situation that we're in with DealHub because it is so convoluted. The CFO is quite unhappy because we have to have an agency.

current_workaround.description: DealHub for quoting with agency management, Excel pricing calculator for pricing logic, manual line item correction in HubSpot, custom formula fields for ARR, contract PDF review for billing terms
current_workaround.adequacy: Failing. RevOps leader reviewing every closed-won deal since January. Working nights on forensic data audits. CFO dissatisfied with agency costs. ARR reporting unreliable.

quotes.quote: One of the things that I'm having to do is go back and review every closed-won deal since the beginning of the year and fix the line items in said deal.
quotes.usage_notes: Visceral description of the manual toll of disconnected quoting tools. Strong pain-point content for RevOps persona targeting DealHub migration.

quotes.quote: That's very much the situation that we're in with DealHub because it is so convoluted. The CFO is quite unhappy because we have to have an agency.
quotes.usage_notes: Two-for-one: captures both the complexity pain and the CFO cost sensitivity. Useful for content targeting both RevOps and Finance personas. Direct DealHub competitive positioning.

quotes.quote: There seems to have been a point in history where they made a different SKU for every deal.
quotes.usage_notes: Relatable for any company that has gone through multiple CRM migrations. Good for content about product library hygiene and CPQ readiness.

quotes.quote: DealHub writes to the product table. It's a one-way sync from DealHub back to HubSpot. I could make changes, but they're gonna get overwritten on the next sync.
quotes.usage_notes: Technical proof of the HubSpot-native advantage. When quoting lives outside HubSpot, RevOps loses control of their own data. Strong differentiator content.

quotes.quote: This leaves me with almost 12,000 line items that I need to go through. It's awesome.
quotes.usage_notes: The sarcasm makes it memorable. Quantified pain point (12,000 line items). Works well in social content and newsletter hooks.

quotes.quote: None of which have start or end date.
quotes.usage_notes: Devastating follow-up to the 12,000 line items quote. Shows cascading data quality failure. Pair with the previous quote for maximum impact.

quotes.quote: I'm having a serious issue with ramp-up deals because ARR is jumbling all together for each year.
quotes.usage_notes: Addresses a common HubSpot limitation. Content angle: how mid-market companies solve ARR reporting when HubSpot's native calculation breaks on complex deal structures.

quotes.quote: I put in the billing start date. I put in the billing end date. Why is it adding everything to ARR so that it's tripling it?
quotes.usage_notes: Specific, reproducible frustration with HubSpot's ARR rollup. High search intent keyword potential. The exasperation is palpable.

quotes.quote: I'm gonna cry, and I think I might have cried last night.
quotes.usage_notes: Raw emotional moment. Use carefully and with empathy. Captures the human cost of broken quoting processes. Good for narrative-style content about RevOps burnout.

quotes.quote: Literally, I am having to go back and read every contract for this year because people put ARR terms in the notes of the PDF.
quotes.usage_notes: Shows the downstream cost of unstructured quoting data. Contract terms in PDF notes instead of structured fields. Strong content angle for data governance and CPQ ROI.

value_language: opportunity to test things out and pilot
